label: SES-030 styling Conversation 2 closeout: 3 decisions (DEC-105..DEC-107), 1 session, 4 references; no PIs

session.title: Styling Conversation 2 — build planning: ui-PRD-v0.6, implementation plan, six slice prompts, version-bundling resolution
session.conversation_reference: Claude.ai conversation on 05-16-26 that opened the styling workstream's build-planning phase against the design-pass document produced by SES-027 earlier the same day. The conversation operated against the project default ARCHITECTURE mode for PRD and implementation-plan authoring, then switched to DETAIL mode (per profile preferences for Claude Code prompt authoring) for the six slice prompts. The conversation produced eight deliverables across the session: ui-PRD-v0.6.md (430 lines), ui-v0.6-implementation-plan.md (456 lines), and six slice prompts at PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/prompts/CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-v2-ui-v0.6-{A..F}-*.md (456 + 369 + 398 + 495 + 369 + 304 lines). Plus pre-flight corrections to PRD and implementation plan reflecting the actual v0.5 codebase shape, and an identifier rebase at close-out time reflecting v0.5 parallel-workstream consumption of anticipated identifiers (SES-028 → SES-030, DEC-095/096/097 → DEC-105/106/107). No transcript preserved per DEC-025.
session.topics_covered:
Seed prompt: not a formal kickoff document — this conversation opened against the styling design pass committed earlier the same day (styling-design-pass.md, output of SES-027) plus the workstream plan at styling-workstream-plan.md. Doug initiated by saying 'lets do the styling conversation' after the prior turn had reconstructed which conversation produced what (he had been confused which conversation gave him 'this script' — turned out to be the styling design pass document, not a script; the conversation had been searching past chats to disambiguate).

Pre-flight. Sparse clone of crmbuilder repo with CLAUDE.md, PRDs/, and crmbuilder-v2/ source. Read CLAUDE.md (684 lines) to ground architecture and conventions. Read styling-workstream-plan.md (227 lines) end to end. Confirmed no styling-conversation-2-kickoff.md existed — working from the workstream plan and design-pass document directly. Verified database state at conversation open: latest session SES-027, latest decision DEC-094, latest PI PI-017. Next anticipated identifiers: SES-028, DEC-095/096/097. (This identifier anticipation later proved wrong; see close-out section.)

What Conversation 2 owed per the workstream plan: (1) settle the version-bundling question — v0.5 bundled vs v0.6 separate; (2) produce a styling release PRD; (3) produce an implementation plan; (4) produce slice build prompts.

Decision 1 — Version bundling (DEC-105). Pause with full eight-element template. Two viable options: bundle the styling work into v0.5 (one release for engagement management + visual design system) versus ship as separate v0.6 release. Option A (bundle) gave one closeout cycle and slice-name collisions could be managed via prefix; Option B (separate) gave two closeout cycles but kept release versions tied to functional themes (the v2 history-navigation index that 'what changed in v0.5' answers cleanly). Doug chose B (separate v0.6). Reasoning: release versions are v2's primary navigation index (git log, README, decisions table, __version__, MCP-exposed status); v2 to date had held one clean functional theme per release (v0.1 storage, v0.2 base CRUD, v0.3 references + sessions write surface, v0.4 polish + methodology panels); bundling would make v0.5 the first v2 release with two unrelated themes welded together, permanently blurring navigability. Cost: one additional closeout cycle (~half day of administrative work). The workstream plan author's weak prior leaned this way and was ratified. Follow-on detail committed in the same decision: PRD file ui-PRD-v0.6.md; implementation plan ui-v0.6-implementation-plan.md; slice prompts under prompts/CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-v2-ui-v0.6-{A..F}-*.md; __version__ after v0.6 closeout '0.6.0'; v0.5 ships first (engagement is currently slightly ahead in planning depth); engagement panel coupling per workstream plan §4's 'v0.5 ships first' branch; design-pass decisions stand as-is.

Decision 2 — Six-slice structure (DEC-106). Surfaced as draft rather than full template (substantively a packaging choice rather than two genuinely different visual outcomes). The workstream plan §5.3 sketched five slices (Foundation / Governance retrofit / Methodology retrofit / Dialog polish / Closeout). Drafted six slices for v0.6: A Foundation + About dialog (canary surface exercising the full token system end-to-end); B Sidebar + master-pane delegate (the shared selected-state vocabulary); C Panel retrofits + ReferencesSection (all 12-13 panels in one omnibus retrofit slice); D Dialogs + form controls; E Status + crash banner (separated from dialogs because crash banner and status surfaces are distinct concerns from dialog chrome); F Closeout. Doug approved as drafted. Three reconciliation differences from the strawman: (1) master-pane delegate pulled to its own slice B rather than buried in panel retrofits — the delegate is shared across all 12+ panels and putting it in panel-retrofit slices would force artificial governance/methodology coupling; (2) governance and methodology retrofits collapsed into single omnibus slice C — the retrofit pattern is identical across all panels, and splitting repeats review work without buying anything once slice B has landed the shared delegate; (3) status surfaces + crash banner promoted to their own slice E — keeping two distinct concerns separately reviewable.

Decision 3 — Slice acceptance pattern (DEC-107). Drafted briefly rather than full template (visual work has different acceptance signals than functional work; the choice was between several reasonable approaches with similar value). Drafted pattern: per-slice after-state screenshot per affected surface, committed to PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/styling-screenshots/slice-{X}/ — captured by Doug after running each slice's Claude Code prompt, used for retrospective ('what did the sidebar look like at the end of slice B?') and as release-note material; plus eyeball verification by Doug against the design-pass document as primary acceptance signal; plus automated WCAG AA contrast check against codified styling.py values at slice F closeout per design pass A9 (the one accessibility commitment v0.6 makes — small Python script using a contrast-ratio library, parameterised over the combinations from design pass §4.4). Doug approved as drafted.

ui-PRD-v0.6.md drafted. 430 lines, format mirroring ui-PRD-v0.4.md. Header (Version 0.6, Last Updated 05-16-26 17:30, Status Draft — pending approval); change log; §1 Overview with Purpose / Background / Source decisions (the full deferral chain DEC-024 through DEC-076 plus the design-pass decisions DEC-087 through DEC-094 plus the three forthcoming decisions to be recorded); §2 Scope with 15 In Scope items and 9 Out of Scope items; §3 Architecture with layer responsibility table (5 new modules, 2 restructured widgets, 12+ panels retoken-touched); §4 Functional Requirements citing the design pass rather than restating values; §5 Cross-Cutting Concerns (version bump, README, test target, screenshot capture protocol); §6 Acceptance Criteria per-slice (A1-A7, B1-B7, C1-C7, D1-D6, E1-E6, F1-F7) plus cumulative roll-up; §7 Implementation Plan Reference with strawman reconciliation; §8 Constraints; §9 Risk Register with 9 risks; §10 Open Questions; §11 Decisions to Be Recorded. Doug approved as drafted.

ui-v0.6-implementation-plan.md drafted. 456 lines, format mirroring ui-v0.4-implementation-plan.md. §1 Overview with strict A→B→C→D→E→F sequential dependency chain (no parallelism); §2 Implementation Choices including the hybrid token-consumption mechanism, font/icon asset bundling under ui/assets/, screenshot capture protocol per DEC-107, WCAG contrast check library choice; §3 Directory tree with ASCII layout showing new modules, restructured widgets, asset directories, and screenshot directory; §4 Build Sequence per-slice deliverables and acceptance gates cross-referenced to PRD ACs; §5 Migration Ordering (explicit 'None'); §6 Test Target (~8-12 new tests in slice F WCAG module; visual verification screenshot-based per DEC-107); §7 Screenshot Capture Protocol (six-step protocol for Doug); §8 Version Source; §9 Closeout Discipline. Doug approved as drafted.

Pre-flight corrections from slice A prompt-drafting. Before authoring slice A's prompt, investigation of the actual v0.5 codebase surfaced three factual errors in the implementation plan and PRD: (1) the v0.1-shipped ui/styling.py module already exists (DEC-024's minimal QSS stub that PI-001 has been tracking) and should be rewritten in place rather than replaced by a new tokens.py — surfaced with full eight-element template since two genuinely different options (rewrite-in-place vs new file + delete legacy) had meaningfully different outcomes; Doug chose Option 2 (rewrite in place) on user-preference grounds plus apply_stylesheet(app) entry-point preservation; (2) About dialog lives at ui/about_dialog.py at the ui/ top level, NOT ui/dialogs/about.py — there is no about.py in dialogs/; (3) ui/base/crud_dialog.py contains BOTH EntityCrudDialog and EntityCrudDeleteDialog (single file with both bases) plus a third dialog base discovered: ui/base/versioned_replace_dialog.py covers CharterReplaceDialog and StatusReplaceDialog. Per-dialog modal-backdrop hook count corrected: 3 base classes + 3 standalone QDialog subclasses = 6 file edits (the bases plus about_dialog.py, dialogs/error.py, dialogs/reference_delete.py). The implementation plan §3 directory tree and §4 slice A deliverables list were patched accordingly; both documents transitioned from 'Draft — pending approval' to 'Approved.'

Mode switch to DETAIL for slice prompt authoring. Per Doug's profile preferences, Claude Code prompt authoring is DETAIL-mode work ('one consequential thing at a time, full discussion, wait for approval before each next step'). Doug confirmed the switch.

Slice A prompt drafted (456 lines). Foundation + About dialog. Nine numbered steps: (1) ui/styling.py rewrite end-to-end with TOKENS dict and accessor/builder; (2) font asset bundling and app-startup loading via QFontDatabase; (3) Lucide icon asset bundling and runtime-tinting loader; (4) modal elevation infrastructure (QGraphicsDropShadowEffect helper + ModalBackdropOverlay widget with attach/detach functions); (5) per-dialog elevation+backdrop hooks across the six files identified; (6) ui/base/list_detail_panel.py token-consuming chrome hooks; (7) app.py startup wiring order; (8) splash screen migration from legacy constants to t() accessor; (9) about_dialog.py re-skin (wordmark + tagline + vertical metadata list). Two upstream architectural choices surfaced via the eight-element template before drafting: (a) token-consumption mechanism (Option A — programmatic QSS generation from styling.py keeping token integrity as a single source of truth, chosen over static QSS file + manual sync burden; follow-on detail: build_app_stylesheet(tokens) function in styling.py, applied in app.py at startup, t(key) accessor for per-widget reads); (b) modal backdrop visibility-coupling mechanism (Option 1 — per-dialog hook via showEvent/hideEvent overrides, chosen over QApplication.focusChanged or global event filter on explicitness/reliability grounds; follow-on: backdrop overlay parented to QMainWindow.centralWidget per PRD Open Question #3). Plus four routine decide-and-announce items: tagline wording ('Declarative CRM deployment and methodology authoring' per design-pass proposal); font file sources (rsms/inter and JetBrains/JetBrainsMono GitHub releases for variable-axis .ttf files); icon loader caching (lazy by (name, size, color_token) key, module-level dict, no invalidation); predecessor slice reference as placeholder ('v2-ui-v0.5-? TBD at v0.6 build-execution time'). Doug approved slice A as drafted.

Slice B prompt drafted (369 lines). Sidebar + master-pane delegate. Eight numbered steps culminating in the shared selected-state vocabulary per DEC-093. One upstream architectural choice surfaced via the eight-element template: master-pane delegate registration mechanism (Option B — centralized via ListDetailPanel base class with per-panel fallback for VersionedPanel-based panels Charter/Status, chosen over per-panel registration on 12 panels which would create a latent bug class for future panels; follow-on: ListDetailPanel.__init__ applies delegate automatically; Topics overrides via class attribute master_pane_delegate_cls = MasterPaneTreeDelegate). Plus three routine items: sidebar implementation via QStyledItemDelegate (per design pass + DEC-093); tree-view variant as subclass MasterPaneTreeDelegate(MasterPaneDelegate); soft-deleted toggle reading via is_soft_deleted_visible callable on the delegate constructor. Doug approved slice B as drafted.

Slice C prompt drafted (398 lines). Panel retrofits + ReferencesSection. Eight numbered steps including an explicit Step 1 inheritance-topology verification pass before any code, pause-and-report if any panel pattern unanticipated. One upstream architectural choice surfaced (brief framing, not full template): label-above form layout scope — slice C owns label-above EVERYWHERE (panels + dialogs via crud_dialog and versioned_replace_dialog bases) via QFormLayout.setRowWrapPolicy(WrapAllRows) plus a QFormLayout QLabel QSS rule in build_app_stylesheet, chosen over splitting between slices C and D. Plus four routine items: ReferencesSection refactored in place preserving public API; kind→label mapping as module-level constant in references_section.py; status combo Valid transitions caption as per-panel QLabel with QSS rule via objectName='statusHintCaption'; engagement panel filename verification (likely engagements.py plural per v0.5 test naming); required-field marker as required_label(text) helper in base/list_detail_panel.py. Doug approved slice C as drafted.

Slice D prompt drafted (495 lines — the largest slice prompt). Dialogs + form controls. Seven numbered steps: button helpers, button category QSS rules, combo dropdown + checkbox indicator, internal context strip, button-category migration across the codebase, delete-confirm body treatment, visual regression smoke. Decide-and-announce items rather than surfaced decisions: button category mechanism as helper functions in base/list_detail_panel.py (primary_button, destructive_button, text_link_button, icon_button) returning QPushButtons with buttonCategory dynamic property; context strip as method on EntityCrudDialog base auto-applied in edit mode only (excluded dialogs verified per design pass §2.7); checkbox custom indicator via QSS background-image referencing pre-tinted Lucide check.svg variants; inline error retoken already covered by slice A; combo dropdown via standard QComboBox QSS. Doug approved slice D as drafted.

Slice E prompt drafted (369 lines). Status, error, warning + crash banner. Six numbered steps including the WarningCallout widget for inline panel warnings, Processes panel retoken from danger-red to warning-amber, error dialog header retoken with Lucide circle-x icon, crash banner re-skin folded into the design system. Decide-and-announce items: WarningCallout as shared helper widget for reusability; crash banner button styling as per-widget setStyleSheet (the banner is exceptional and doesn't fit the five design-system button categories); error dialog header as layout restructure with leading icon row + title in heading-3 danger-text. Doug approved slice E as drafted.

Slice F prompt drafted (304 lines). Closeout. Five numbered steps: __version__ bump to '0.6.0'; README v0.6 release note; WCAG contrast test module using wcag-contrast-ratio library with hand-rolled fallback (parameterized over 8 combinations from design pass §4.4 A9); full regression test pass; final integration smoke checklist covering every surface across slices A-E. Recommended warning-amber token adjustment from #B0731A to #9D6517 if the warning-on-white combination fails AA at body size (closes PRD Open Question #4). Doug approved slice F as drafted.

Close-out artifact authoring. Per the SES-025/026/027 close-out pattern, this conversation's own close-out produces a JSON payload and apply prompt for application via apply_close_out.py. Pre-flight against the database snapshot at close-out time surfaced that v0.5 parallel work had consumed the identifiers this conversation anticipated: latest applied session SES-029 (not SES-027), latest applied decision DEC-104 (not DEC-094). Per the parallel-workstream coupling discipline in DEC-076, this conversation rebased: SES-028 → SES-030 (next available after v0.5's SES-029); DEC-095 → DEC-105, DEC-096 → DEC-106, DEC-097 → DEC-107 (next available after v0.5's DEC-104). Rebase applied across PRD, implementation plan, and all six slice prompts via sed; change-log entries added to PRD and implementation plan documenting the rebase. Same pattern as the SES-027 rebase from SES-026 that occurred during styling Conversation 1's close-out. ses_030.json (this payload) and CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-apply-close-out-ses-030.md authored alongside.
session.artifacts_produced: Eight documents committed to PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/ across multiple commits on origin/main: (1) ui-PRD-v0.6.md (430 lines, new) — the styling release PRD; (2) ui-v0.6-implementation-plan.md (456 lines, new) — companion to the PRD; (3) prompts/CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-v2-ui-v0.6-A-foundation.md (456 lines, new) — slice A prompt; (4) prompts/CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-v2-ui-v0.6-B-sidebar-and-master-pane.md (369 lines, new); (5) prompts/CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-v2-ui-v0.6-C-panel-retrofits.md (398 lines, new); (6) prompts/CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-v2-ui-v0.6-D-dialogs-and-form-controls.md (495 lines, new); (7) prompts/CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-v2-ui-v0.6-E-status-and-crash-banner.md (369 lines, new); (8) prompts/CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-v2-ui-v0.6-F-closeout.md (304 lines, new). Plus this close-out payload close-out-payloads/ses_030.json and apply prompt prompts/CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-apply-close-out-ses-030.md. Plus pre-flight document patches (commit 4ef8700 — styling.py rename, about_dialog path correction, dialog inheritance topology corrections) and an identifier rebase (commit 7255667 — SES-028→SES-030, DEC-095/096/097→DEC-105/106/107 across 6 files). No PI patches — all v0.6 build work folds into existing PI-001 (parent for the whole styling pass, already reopened as parallel workstream by DEC-076).
session.in_flight_at_end: v0.5 build is active in parallel — by close-out time v0.5 had advanced to its slice A prompt (engagement panel followup launcher wiring per CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-v2-ui-v0.5-A-followup-launcher-wiring.md committed by v0.5 work) and consumed identifiers through SES-029 + DEC-104. The two workstreams remain coupled only at v0.5's engagement panel per DEC-076's boundary discipline; slice C's panel-retrofit work will pick up the engagement panel alongside the other 12 existing panels when v0.6 build executes. After SES-030 apply lands, v0.6 build executes via the six slice prompts in sequence (A→B→C→D→E→F, strict ordering, no parallelism within v0.6). v0.5 ships first per DEC-105; v0.6 ships when its six slices complete. Status entity versioned-replace from 'v0.5 complete' to 'v0.6 complete' is operator-authored after slice F lands. The paper-test (DEC-077) stays deferred until v0.5 ships and a CBM engagement record exists — unchanged by v0.6 work.

decisions.title: Version bundling for the styling work: ship as separate v0.6 release, not bundled into v0.5
decisions.context: The styling workstream's build planning needed to settle a question the workstream plan §6 named explicitly: whether the styling work ships bundled into v0.5 (one release containing engagement management + visual design system) or as a separate v0.6 release that lands shortly after v0.5. Both releases were in flight at the time of this conversation — v0.5's engagement management was in architectural planning, the styling design pass had just closed (SES-027), and Conversation 2 (this conversation) was opening to plan the styling build. The decision had to be made before slice file naming, PRD file naming, or implementation plan file naming could be settled — those depend on whether the styling work is v0.5 slices E-I or v0.6 slices A-F. The workstream plan author's weak prior leaned toward separate v0.6.
decisions.decision: **Ship as a separate v0.6 release.** v0.5 ships engagement management alone (slices A-D, four slices) and closes out independently when engagement work completes; __version__ bumps to '0.5.0' and v0.5 closes via its own session-record + apply prompt. Styling builds in parallel under CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-v2-ui-v0.6-{X}-*.md with its own A-F six-slice ordering. When styling completes, __version__ bumps to '0.6.0' and v0.6 closes out. Two README sections, two closeouts. Engagement-panel coupling resolved per workstream plan §4 'v0.5 ships first' branch — slice C's panel retrofit work picks up the engagement panel alongside the other 12 existing panels. Follow-on detail committed in the same decision turn: styling release PRD at ui-PRD-v0.6.md; implementation plan at ui-v0.6-implementation-plan.md; slice prompts under prompts/CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-v2-ui-v0.6-{A..F}-*.md; __version__ after v0.6 closeout '0.6.0'; release order v0.5 first then v0.6; v0.5 engagement panel inherits whatever design tokens have shipped at slice-land time; design-pass decisions (DEC-087..DEC-094) stand as-is.
decisions.rationale: Release versions are v2's primary navigation index — git log, README, decisions table, __version__, MCP-exposed status all index by version. To date v2 has held one clean functional theme per release: v0.1 storage system, v0.2 base CRUD, v0.3 references + sessions write surface, v0.4 polish + methodology panels. Bundling makes v0.5 the first v2 release with two unrelated themes welded together, which permanently blurs the project's navigability — anyone (including Doug in a year) asking 'what changed in v0.5' gets a two-track answer where the simple cases have been giving one-track answers. Methodology rule at stake: the workstream plan §6 already named 'release timing' and 'closeout coordination' as the considerations, and the author's weak prior leaned toward separate. The design pass document didn't pick either way. The bundling cost (two unrelated themes under one version) is permanent in the project's history; the separate-release cost (one additional closeout cycle, roughly half a day of administrative work) is real but bounded. Functional independence of the two workstreams is load-bearing.
decisions.alternatives_considered: **Option A — Bundle into v0.5.** Slice prompts go under CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-v2-ui-v0.5-{X}-*.md and interleave with engagement-management slices (estimated v0.5 slices: A-D engagement management + E-I styling = nine slices). One v0.5 release ships when BOTH workstreams complete — the slower one gates the release. One README section. One closeout. Rejected on navigability grounds: bundling permanently blurs the release-version index that v2 uses for git log, README, decisions navigation, and MCP status reporting. The marginal savings (one fewer closeout cycle, ~half day) didn't compensate for the permanent blurring cost.
decisions.consequences: Slice prompts named under CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-v2-ui-v0.6-{A..F}-*.md. PRD at ui-PRD-v0.6.md; implementation plan at ui-v0.6-implementation-plan.md. __version__ in crmbuilder-v2/src/crmbuilder_v2/__init__.py bumped to '0.6.0' at slice F closeout (slice F handles the bump). Two README release-note sections — v0.5 entry plus v0.6 entry. Two closeouts — v0.5 closes out independently when its slices land; v0.6 closes out via slice F + the post-slice-F operator steps (status versioned-replace, SES-030 application). Engagement-panel coupling: v0.5 ships first; v0.5's engagement panel ships with Qt-default styling; v0.6 slice C picks up the engagement panel alongside the other 12 existing panels in the omnibus retrofit slice. Build-planning decisions taken in this conversation (DEC-106, DEC-107) are recorded at this same close-out (SES-030).

decisions.title: Slice structure for v0.6: six slices A-F reconciling the workstream-plan strawman
decisions.context: After DEC-105 settled the version-bundling question (separate v0.6), the styling build needed a slice structure. The workstream plan §5.3 had sketched a five-slice strawman (Foundation / Governance retrofit / Methodology retrofit / Dialog polish / Closeout). The design-pass document §3.3 had a more granular eight-step suggested order. These needed reconciliation, but the design pass already conceded 'approximate; Conversation 2 may restructure slice boundaries based on file-conflict topology with v0.5 work running in parallel.' The decision was about how to package the work — same surfaces, same tokens, same components either way, but different review-burden distribution and different file-conflict risk profiles.
decisions.decision: **Six slices A-F.** Slice A: Foundation + About dialog (token module, font and icon asset bundling, loader helpers, modal elevation infrastructure, base widget hooks, About dialog re-skin as the canary surface). Slice B: Sidebar + master-pane delegate (the shared selected-state vocabulary per DEC-093, applied via custom QStyledItemDelegate on the sidebar QListWidget and via a shared QStyledItemDelegate subclass registered on every panel's master view). Slice C: Panel retrofits + ReferencesSection (omnibus retrofit covering all 12 existing panels plus v0.5's engagement panel; label-above form layout EVERYWHERE via QFormLayout.WrapAllRows; ReferencesSection sub-sectioned plain-list rewrite). Slice D: Dialogs + form controls (five button categories with full state coverage via dynamic buttonCategory property; internal context strip on record-editing dialogs; combo dropdown popup + checkbox custom indicator; delete-confirm body treatment). Slice E: Status, error, warning + crash banner (WarningCallout widget; Processes panel retoken from danger-red to warning-amber; error dialog header retoken; crash banner folded into the design system). Slice F: Closeout (version bump, README, WCAG contrast test, regression pass, integration smoke). Strictly sequential dependency chain A→B→C→D→E→F; no parallelism within v0.6 because each slice depends on tokens, hooks, or categories established by prior slices.
decisions.rationale: Three reconciliation differences from the workstream plan §5.3 strawman: (1) the master-pane delegate is pulled to its own slice B rather than buried in panel-retrofit slices — the delegate is shared across all 12+ panels; putting it in panel-retrofit slices would artificially couple governance and methodology retrofit work (one of them would land the delegate and the other would inherit it); pulling it forward to its own slice lets slice C be a single omnibus retrofit instead of two; (2) governance and methodology retrofits collapsed into one slice C — the retrofit pattern is identical across all 12 panels; splitting it repeats the same review work twice without buying anything once slice B has landed the shared delegate; (3) status surfaces + crash banner promoted to their own slice E — the workstream plan would have folded these into 'dialog polish' (slice D), but the crash banner currently lives outside the design system and the inline-error treatment cuts across many panels; bundling with dialogs muddles two different concerns. The sequencing also accounts for the engagement panel coupling: v0.5 ships first per DEC-105, so by slice C's land time the engagement panel exists and is picked up by the omnibus retrofit alongside the other 12 panels.
decisions.alternatives_considered: **Option A — Stay close to the workstream plan five-slice strawman (Foundation / Governance retrofit / Methodology retrofit / Dialog polish / Closeout).** Rejected on three grounds: master-pane delegate buried in panel-retrofit slices creates artificial governance/methodology coupling; redundant review work in two parallel retrofit slices doing the same pattern; status and crash banner work muddled into dialog-polish slice. **Option B — Use the design pass §3.3 eight-step ordering more directly (Foundation, Sidebar, Master pane delegate, Governance retrofit, Methodology retrofit, Dialog polish, Status/warning + crash banner, Closeout).** Rejected because the design pass author already acknowledged the ordering as approximate; collapsing it to six slices reduces closeout overhead without losing material structure. **Option C — Hybrid with reserved override mechanism.** Rejected on YAGNI grounds at decision time (later partially rewalked back: the Topics-panel tree-view delegate override in slice B used a class-attribute override pattern, justified pragmatically because Topics is a concrete v0.6 caller, not speculative).
decisions.consequences: Slice prompts authored at PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/prompts/CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-v2-ui-v0.6-{A..F}-*.md, lengths 456 / 369 / 398 / 495 / 369 / 304 lines respectively. Per-slice acceptance criteria specified in ui-PRD-v0.6.md §6 (A1-A7, B1-B7, C1-C7, D1-D6, E1-E6, F1-F7) plus cumulative roll-up. The strictly sequential dependency chain means slice F cannot start until slice E lands; slice E cannot start until slice D lands; etc. The engagement panel coupling between v0.5 and v0.6 is resolved in slice C: by the time slice C runs, v0.5 has shipped per DEC-105 and v0.5's engagement panel is part of the omnibus retrofit. Per-slice screenshot capture protocol per DEC-107 applied across slices A-E; slice F adds the automated WCAG contrast verification.

decisions.title: Slice acceptance pattern for v0.6 visual work: per-slice screenshots + closeout WCAG check
decisions.context: v0.6 is visual work with different acceptance signals than functional work. For v0.4 and prior, 'slice done' meant tests pass and new functionality works. For visual surfaces, the test-pass signal is weaker (visual rendering is hard to assert in unit tests without expensive snapshot infrastructure), and eyeball verification by Doug is more meaningful. The styling Conversation 2 needed to settle the per-slice and per-release acceptance pattern.
decisions.decision: **Per-slice after-state screenshots plus eyeball verification, plus a single automated WCAG AA contrast check at slice F closeout.** Each visual slice (A through E) commits after-state screenshots of each affected surface to PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/styling-screenshots/slice-{X}/. Screenshots captured by Doug after running the slice's Claude Code prompt; committed in a separate operator commit immediately after the prompt's commit lands. Filename convention {surface-name}.png (e.g., about-dialog.png, sidebar.png, master-pane-table.png, master-pane-tree.png, master-pane-soft-deleted.png, sessions-panel.png, etc.). Total expected count across v0.6: ~15-20 PNGs at ~100-300 KB each, ~3-5 MB committed to git. Slice F adds an automated WCAG AA contrast verification test at tests/crmbuilder_v2/ui/test_token_contrast.py using the wcag-contrast-ratio Python library (with hand-rolled fallback if the library is unavailable), parameterized over the text-on-background combinations from design pass §4.4 (A9). The WCAG check is a build gate per this decision — failures are not tolerated; if any combination fails, a token-value adjustment lands in ui/styling.py and slice F re-runs.
decisions.rationale: For a solo developer with the right aesthetic eye and Doug's three-4K-monitor workstation context, eyeball verification is the appropriate primary signal for visual work — automated pixel-measurement or QSS-introspection checks would be overkill at this scale. Screenshots become project history and release-note material: a year from now, asking 'what did the sidebar look like at the end of slice B?' has a concrete answer in styling-screenshots/slice-B/sidebar.png. Per-slice (rather than only release-final) screenshots also support retrospective work — if a v0.7+ regression is suspected, the slice-by-slice progression is visible in git history. The single automated check (WCAG contrast) is the one accessibility commitment v0.6 makes per design pass A9; running it once at slice F closeout is sufficient because token values are read-only after slice A merges (per PRD §8 constraint). The git-tracked storage cost (~3-5 MB) is the intended trade for project navigability.
decisions.alternatives_considered: **Automated pixel-measurement or QSS-introspection checks.** Rejected as overkill at v0.6's scale — building snapshot test infrastructure or QSS-property assertions adds significant code without buying meaningful regression catch beyond what eyeball + screenshots already provide. **Eyeball-only without screenshots.** Rejected on history-loss grounds — if no screenshots are committed, retrospective work has no concrete reference for 'what changed visually in slice X' beyond the design pass document and the code diff. **Screenshots gitignored.** Rejected: defeats the retrospective purpose. **Multiple WCAG runs (per-slice rather than closeout-only).** Rejected because tokens don't change between slices (read-only after slice A per PRD §8); one run at slice F is sufficient.
decisions.consequences: PRD §6 acceptance criteria for each visual slice (A1-A7, B1-B7, C1-C7, D1-D6, E1-E6) include the screenshot-commit requirement; PRD §6 slice F includes the WCAG check requirement (F4). Implementation plan §7 specifies the six-step screenshot capture protocol Doug follows after running each slice's Claude Code prompt. Each slice's commit message template instructs Claude Code to 'notify Doug for screenshot capture' as the final step. Slice F's prompt-runner picks the specific WCAG library (wcag-contrast-ratio recommended) and authors tests/crmbuilder_v2/ui/test_token_contrast.py parameterized over the design pass §4.4 combinations. If the warning-on-white combination fails AA at body size (design pass §4.4 flagged it as borderline at caption size), the recommended remediation is a token-value adjustment from #B0731A to #9D6517 — closes PRD Open Question #4.
